content: Images of consonants of the English alphabet (Capitals) are observed in a mirror. What is the number of images of these which do not look like their original shapes ?
explanation:
To solve this problem, we need to:

  1. Identify all the capital consonants in the English alphabet.

  2. Determine which of these consonants look the same when observed in a mirror (i.e., have vertical reflectional symmetry).

  3. Count the number of consonants that do not look like their original shapes after reflection.

Step 1: List all capital consonants.

The English alphabet has 26 capital letters. The vowels are A, E, I, O, U.

So, the consonants are all letters except these vowels.

Consonants: B, C, D, F, G, H, J, K, L, M, N, P, Q, R, S, T, V, W, X, Y, Z.

Total number of consonants = $26 - 5 = 21$.

Step 2: Identify consonants that look the same in a mirror.

A letter looks the same in a mirror if it has vertical reflectional symmetry. Let's check each consonant:

  • B: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • C: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • D: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • F: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • G: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • H: Looks the same (has vertical symmetry).

  • J: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • K: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • L: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • M: Looks the same (has vertical symmetry).

  • N: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • P: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • Q: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • R: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • S: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

  • T: Looks the same (has vertical symmetry).

  • V: Looks the same (has vertical symmetry).

  • W: Looks the same (has vertical symmetry).

  • X: Looks the same (has vertical symmetry).

  • Y: Looks the same (has vertical symmetry).

  • Z: Does not look the same (looks reversed).

The consonants that look the same in a mirror are: H, M, T, V, W, X, Y.

There are 7 such consonants.

Step 3: Count the consonants that do not look like their original shapes.

Total number of consonants = 21.

Number of consonants that look the same in a mirror = 7.

Number of consonants that do not look the same in a mirror = Total consonants - Consonants that look the same

$$ = 21 - 7 = 14 $$

The consonants that do not look like their original shapes when observed in a mirror are: B, C, D, F, G, J, K, L, N, P, Q, R, S, Z. There are 14 such consonants.

The final answer is $\boxed{\text{14}}$.
